headline: Remains of teen Fil-Am found dead in New Jersey to be brought back to PH | ABS-CBN News
text:
The remains of the missing Filipino-American teen found dead in Newark Bay, New Jersey will be brought back home to the Philippines, her family said.
The body of 18-year-old Hayley Erin “Lee” Avestruz Alvarado was pulled out of Newark Bay in Bayonne, New Jersey on March 5.  
According to her father Stephen, they will hold a wake for Hayley on March 18, while funeral services and cremation will be held on March 19 in Jersey City.
The Alvarado family will then bring Hayley's remains back home to their loved ones in the Philippines.
In an earlier interview with ABS-CBN News, Stephen said on December 17, Hayley was seen in their security camera recording leaving their home past midnight, wearing her back pack. That was the last time they saw her alive.  
Hayley was a freshman at Montclair University studying language engineering, majoring in linguistics. She graduated summa cum laude in high school.  
“Si Haley, talagang matalino siya sa school, focused talaga siya sa pag-aaral, nakikita ko sa kanya yung mga achievements niya gusto niya talaga palagi number one siya sa klase,” Stephen said.
He also admitted he was so busy at work that he wished he had spent more time with his daughter.  
“Ang nangyayayri sa amin dito, Trabaho, trabaho, trabaho -- I can say na may pagkukulang ako na, hindi ko sila mabigyan ng oras,” he said, "Kung ibabalik ulit, gusto kong yakapin siya araw araw,” Stephen said.
He also told ABS-CBN News that based on Hayley’s search history on her computer about bus routes, she may have taken the bus after leaving their home on December 17.  
Stephen said that according to investigators, Hayley was last seen in security cameras in South Kearny, near the Hackensack River -- just 17 minutes away from their Jersey City home, if she took the NJ Transit bus number 1.    
The part of Hackensack River where she was last seen is about 4.6 miles away upstream from Newark Bay -- where her body was recently found.  
Authorities said there were no visible signs of trauma on her body.  
The cause of death is still pending, as the family is still waiting for the autopsy report from the medical examiner.
Missing 18-year-old FilAm found dead in NJ bay
RELATED VIDEO

headline: ‘Does it have an ECC or not?’ DENR asks Sagbayan LGU on Chocolate Hills resort | ABS-CBN News
text:
SAGBAYAN, Bohol — Following claims made by the Municipality of Sagbayan that it did not receive a copy of the temporary closure order issued by the Department of Environment and Natural Resources (DENR) that led to the issuance of a Business Permit in January, the environment chief on Friday rebutted with a question of whether or not the establishment had an environmental compliance certificate.
In a virtual press conference, DENR Secretary Toni Yulo Loyzaga urged the local government to remain focused on the resort’s violation instead of focusing on whether they received the notice.
“The issue is does it have an ECC or not. It’s not whether hindi naman kami nabigyan ng kopya. Alam naman po siguro natin at the local level kung ano ang kailangan masubmit bago mag issue ng isang building permit sa protected area,” she said.
Manager of controversial Chocolate Hills resort admits not being able to acquire environment certificate
(It’s not whether they were not given a copy. I think they know at the local level what the requirements that need to be fulfilled are before issuing a building permit on a protected area.)
Loyzaga added the agency learned that Captain’s Peak Resort was first granted a building permit by the LGU in 2020.
At that time, according to Loyzaga, the LGU already knew the resort didn’t have an ECC.
Viral Chocolate Hills resort ordered closed due to violation: DENR
“The second building permit was issued on September 20, 2022. Alam naman natin siguro at nila na wala silang in-apply-an ng ECC. So patuloy po yang ini-issue-han ng local government. At alam naman nila siguro na while they were building all of these, they did not apply for the ECC.”
(I think they know that the resort has not applied for an ECC. So the local government continued to issue permits. I think they know that while the proponents of the project were building all of these, they did not apply for the ECC.)
The DENR said that together with the LGU, it has the obligation to monitor the area.
Following the incident, they admitted that better coordination is needed with local governments and regional offices and other stakeholders.
Loyzaga said that an evaluation of systems and personnel are currently being done.
She said it will result “in some changes in the line up of team members on the ground.”
LACK OF ECC NOT ONLY VIOLATION
Environment Undersecretary Juan Miguel Cuna said the Captain’s Peak Resorts operation without an ECC was not the only violation it has committed.
He said it may also be subject to hefty fines as it also lacked other registration requirements.
“Nagdi-discharge ng tubig without a discharge permit, there’s a corresponding fine. For failure to register as a hazardous waste generator, may fine po ‘yun,” Cuna said.
“Under the E-NIPAS, for criminal liability may mga penalty po siya. Minimum of P1 million to P5 million maximum for criminal liability. Minimum of 6 to maximum of 12 years for putting up structures without the permits within the protected area. Meron ding administrative fines na pwedeng i-impose sa kanila, starts at P50,000 and a max of P5 million.”
He added DENR personnel who visited and inspected the resort on Thursday found that the establishment was using a deep well.  
“We’ve checked with the National Water Resources Board (NWRB) if they have obtained a permit for this or whether they have a pending application. The NWRB has already issued a certification that they don’t have a permit neither do they have an application for this.”
As of now, it remains unclear whether Captain’s Peak Resort will be allowed to reopen.
Following the row, the DENR emphasized that it is not singling out any establishment.
It has also given instructions to check other establishments within the area of the Chocolate Hills.

headline: Marcos Jr, Blinken to discuss South China Sea issue | ABS-CBN News
text:
President Ferdinand Marcos Jr said Thursday that his upcoming discussions with US Secretary of State Antony Blinken will delve on “maintaining peace in the South China Sea”.
Foreign Affairs Secretary Enrique Manalo earlier confirmed that Secretary Blinken will meet with President Marcos on March 19.
“With an eye not to winning any kind of conflict but really just to maintain the peace and to continue to defend the sovereignty and the sovereign rights of the Philippines, when it comes to these international differences [that] we are having to deal with right now,” President Marcos said in a joint press conference with Czech Republic President Petr Pavel.



Marcos said discussing the South China Sea issue with Blinken is necessary in matters concerning international trade.
“It is an issue that is not only important for those surrounded the region, the Indo-Pacific region, for ASEAN, and for the members states of ASEAN that surround the South China Sea,” he said.
“But also, because this is a safe navigation and passage of the South China Sea is important to international trade, as close to 60% of international trade goes to that channel and we hope to continue these discussions,” he added.



China claims almost the entire South China Sea and has sought to assert sovereignty there despite competing claims from Southeast Asian nations and an international arbitration ruling that its stance has no legal basis.
Tensions between Beijing and the Philippines have flared in recent months as ships from both countries have clashed near contested reefs, with the most recent dispute taking place this week.

With Agence France-Presse

headline: Quiboloy camp responds to Senate show cause, explains why he should not be arrested | ABS-CBN News
text:
Apollo Quiboloy, head of "the Kingdom of Jesus Christ," a non-Catholic religious group and spiritual adviser of president-elect Rodrigo Duterte, speaks during a press conference in Davao City in southern island of Mindanao on May 23, 2016. Manman Dejeto, AFP/File 
The camp of Pastor Apollo Quiboloy on Friday submitted its response to a show cause order on why he should not be detained by the Senate for skipping hearings on the allegations of sexual abuse against him.
In a 19-page document, Quiboloy's lawyer Melanio Elvis Balayan argued that the investigation "is not in aid of legislation but is tantamount to usurpation of judicial functions beyond the powers of the Senate.” 
He added that the resolution that sought the probe was “brazenly incriminatory” and had “categorically declared the guilt of Pastor Quiboloy for crimes being attributed to him, totally disregarding the constitutional presumption of innocence until proven guilty beyond reasonable doubt.”
The lawyer said Quiboloy's refusal to attend the hearing was “not a sign of disrespect to honorable Senate nor a disregard of its powers and functions but is purely based on the fact that the criminal accusations against him can only be legitimately settled before the competent courts.”
“We still believe po na yung resolution itself is incriminatory and therefore useless po para kay Pastor Quiboloy na siya ay pumunta sa Senado without sacrificing his fundamental bill of rights... Yung resolution mismo po ay sinasabi na guilty na po sa mga krimen si Pastor at ito ay patuloy niya na kino-commit,” Balayan said.
"Pangalawa po, nabanggit din po namin doon na may limitasyon ang karapatan ng Kongreso sa pag-iimbestiga at ang limitasyon na yun ay, precisely, to regulate or to control the legislator from abusing the fundamental bill of rights of resource persons or witnesses appealing thereat," he said.
Asked if Quiboloy would not appear before the Senate, Balayan said, “Hanggat nakasalang po ang aming Compliance and Show Cause Order, hihintayin po namin ang tugon ng Senado sa aming Legal Compliance at from there we will take it po kung ano ang the best legal avenue or legal actions we could take.”
“Sana po ay basahin nang mabuti ng ating mga kagalanggalang na senador yung mga legal justifications po na isinalang namin sapagkat ito po ay naaayon sa… existing jurisprudence on the powers of the Congress or the Senate in particular to conduct investigation po without violating the sacred bill of rights of resource persons or witnesses po na umaattend sa kanilang mga pagdinig,” he added. 
The office of Sen. Risa Hontiveros, who is leading the investigation, received the response of Quiboloy's camp at around 8:40 a.m., within the 48-hour window given for them to justify why he should not be detained.
US judge orders unsealing of Quiboloy arrest warrant, indictment 
Quiboloy, founder of the religious group Kingdom of Jesus Christ (KOJC), recently denied that he sexually abused female personal assistants or "pastorals", saying the women made up the accusations because he rejected them.
"Ako po ay hindi nag-asawa. Ngayon inaakusahan ako ng napakaraming babae. Eto po ang kasalanan ko. Pinayaman ako ng Panginoon. Akala nila sa akin, single ako, kaya pinag-aagawan ako. Pagkatapos na ako'y maghihinde, mapapahiya, ibabaliktad nila sa akin. 'Yan ang tinatawag kong Potiphar's wife syndrome," he said.
Quiboloy faces a separate House of Representatives investigation on the alleged franchise violations of Sonshine Media Network International (SMNI), a media outlet associated with him.
House lawmakers urge Quiboloy to face SMNI probe

headline: Cancer patients in the Philippines falling for alternative 'cures' | ABS-CBN News
text:
Mary Ann Eduarte talks during an interview with AFP, at her home in Montalban, Rizal, November 20, 2023. - A shortage of doctors, difficulties of reaching a hospital in the archipelago, poor health literacy and fear of incurring huge medical bills have led many people suffering chronic conditions to seek alternative treatments online. Jam Sta. Rosa, AFP/File  
Filipino single mother Mary Ann Eduarte delayed chemotherapy for her breast cancer for several years and instead took food supplements falsely promoted on social media as cures for the deadly disease.
They didn't work and the cancer spread to her lungs and bones.
Eduarte is one of many Filipinos duped by medical misinformation flooding the social media platforms where they rank among the world's heaviest users.
A shortage of doctors, the difficulty of reaching a hospital in the archipelago, poor health literacy, and fear of incurring huge medical bills have led many people suffering from chronic conditions to seek alternative treatments online.
In recent years, AFP digital investigation journalists have seen an explosion in the volume of posts and paid advertisements promoting unproven treatments for diseases such as cancer.
The trend was fueled by the COVID-19 pandemic, when healthcare systems were overwhelmed and many were too scared to visit a hospital.
Eduarte, 47, who makes a living selling beauty products online, found a lump in her right breast during a self-check in 2014.
She was advised to have a biopsy to find out if it was cancer, but she was scared and delayed having the procedure for two years.
Instead, she spent about 50,000 pesos ($900) a month on food supplements, including drinks made from tropical fruit and barley grass that she had seen advertised as cancer cures on Facebook and YouTube.
Eduarte finally agreed to have the biopsy in 2016, which confirmed the tumour was malignant.
But she refused chemotherapy, fearing it would make her sick and lose her hair, and continued taking the supplements for another three years.
"I really believed they would destroy my cancer cells because that's what I was being told by their marketing," Eduarte told AFP at her home near Manila.
"They were posting testimonials that said people were being cured."
After her cancer metastasised, Eduarte agreed to chemotherapy.
"I made the wrong decision," she admitted.
"Those food supplements actually cost me more than if I had immediately sought standard medical treatment."
Gov't targets free breast cancer tests by June

'OUR POWERS ARE LIMITED'

Madonna Realuyo, an oncologist at the Bicol Regional Hospital and Medical Center in the central Philippines, said online misinformation about cancer treatment was a "serious problem".
"Five out of 10 patients I see ask me about something they have seen or read on the internet -- 90 percent of the time the information is incorrect," Realuyo said.
"Telling them the correct information does not guarantee that they will listen to us or believe us."
The cost of cancer treatment, which can reach millions of pesos, made patients vulnerable to deceptive marketing of unproven products that are supposedly cheaper.
"Once you're diagnosed with cancer, the reality is that there's a lot of out-of-pocket expenses," said Aileen Antolin of the Philippine Foundation for Breast Cancer.
AFP has a global team of journalists who debunk misinformation as part of the third-party fact-checking programme of Meta, the parent company of Facebook. Fact-checkers from around 90 organisations, including media outlets, check posts on Facebook, WhatsApp and Instagram.
AFP has repeatedly debunked posts on Facebook that have falsely promoted products as natural cures for cancer, including "Doc Atoie's Finest Guyabano Wine", which was one of the products used by Eduarte.
The drink was featured in hundreds of posts that were shared on Facebook pages with tens, even hundreds, of thousands of followers.
The Philippines' Food and Drug Administration (FDA) told AFP it was not able to go after companies or individuals falsely promoting products online because it still doesn't have guidelines for implementing this section of the 2009 law that established the agency.
Instead, it issues warnings on its website and social media pages.
"Our powers are limited," FDA lawyer Pamela Sevilla told AFP.
Facebook owner Meta's ad policy prohibits any "promises or suggestions of unrealistic outcomes" for "health, weight loss or economic opportunity".
It says ads for over-the-counter medicines should comply with licences and approvals required by local laws.
These ads can be removed from the platform once flagged, while posts that do not directly violate Meta's community standards but are rated as false by third-party fact-checkers like AFP are labelled as misinformation and demoted so they are less likely to appear in newsfeeds.
But when AFP checked Meta's ad library it found ads for "Doc Atoie's Finest Guyabano Wine" and some other products debunked by AFP still there.
Heart attack, diabetes, cancer among top causes of death in PH: DOH

SILENCED BY LAWSUITS 

The barrage of medical misinformation during the pandemic prompted Melbourne-based doctor Adam Smith, who speaks Tagalog, to make YouTube videos identifying misleading ads or posts.
"I realised a huge number of people in the population believed they could treat their medical illness with supplements and vitamins, which was crazy to me," Smith told AFP via Zoom.
But he quit after being hit with several lawsuits by the companies whose products he was exposing.
"These companies and individuals are very happy to use the Philippine justice system to silence free speech and to silence any criticism," Smith said.
After surviving cancer, Eduarte said she was now on a mission to educate others about the dangers of online misinformation.
"I'm telling you, having taken those food supplements... they really did nothing to cure my illness," she said.
Putin says Russia is close to making cancer vaccine

© Agence France-Presse

headline: Czech president, bukas sa mas malalim na kooperasyon sa Pilipinas | ABS-CBN News

Nagsimula namang makatanggap ng tulong ang mga magsasakang naapektuhan ng matinding tagtuyot. Nagpa-Patrol, Dennis Datu. TV Patrol, Biyernes, 15 Marso 2024

headline: Closure order sa Captain’s Peak Resort ipinatupad ng DENR | ABS-CBN News

Nagsalita na rin sila kaugnay ng pahayag ng LGU sa hindi nito pagkakatanggap ng kopya ng closure order, dahilan para mabigyan nila ng business permit ang kontrobersiyal na resort. Nagpa-Patrol, Raphael Bosano. TV Patrol, Biyernes, 15 Marso 2024

headline: Marcos Jr.: PH not instigating disputes in South China Sea | ABS-CBN News
text:
A Chinese Coast Guard vessel uses a water cannon on a BFAR ship during its resupply mission to the Panatag Shoal. ABS-CBN News/file
PRAGUE—President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. on Friday denied that the country has made actions to provoke disputes in the South China Sea.
 This follows the statements of Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesman Wang Wenbin who urged the Philippines to “stop misleading the international community, stop instigating disputes using the South China Sea issue, and stop drawing support from external forces to disrupt peace and stability in the region.”
 “Well I don’t know of any instance where the Philippines has instigated anything, at any point, both verbally, or militarily, or diplomatically,” Marcos Jr. told reporters.
“We were busy, we are busy, we have been busy in running the country and making the best life of Filipinos,” he added.
Marcos also insisted that the Philippines “did not begin all these problems” and the commotions were not caused by the country.
“I don’t know what they are referring to,” he said.
Marcos also stressed that the country’s sovereignty and sovereign rights will remain as the country's priorities when it considers any possible exploration in the West Philippine Sea.
He made the remarks when asked if he is open to reviving the negotiation for joint oil exploration with China following the supposed discovery of 100 million tons of oil in the South China Sea by the China National Offshore Oil Corporation (CNOOC).
“We cannot at any point somehow compromise the territorial integrity of the Philippines,” he said.
“So that is going to be the main principle behind any kind of talks that we might have. So depending on areas that we are talking about, that will come into play. So let me leave it at that.  That is the guiding principle that I’m following whenever it comes to all of these things,” he said.
RELATED VIDEO

headline: Kampo ni Quiboloy, sinagot ang show cause order ng Senado | ABS-CBN News
text: Naghain na ng sagot ang kampo ni Kingdom of Jesus Christ founder Apollo Quiboloy sa ipinalabas na show cause order ng Senado. Sa kabila nito, gusto pa rin ni Senador Risa Hontiveros na maaresto ang religious leader. Nagpa-Patrol, Willard Cheng. TV Patrol, Biyernes, 15 Marso 2024

headline: Suspensyon sa 23 empleyado ng NFA, binawi ng Ombudsman | ABS-CBN News

Pero paglilinaw ng Ombudsman, hindi nangangahulugan na abswelto na ang mga ito. Nagpa-Patrol, Victoria Tulad. TV Patrol, Biyernes, 15 Marso 2024

headline: Imbakan ng plastic waste sa Quezon City, nasunog | ABS-CBN News
text: Tatlo ang sugatan sa sunog sa Valenzuela City, isang tindahan ang nasunog sa Taytay, Rizal, habang natupok ang imbakan ng plastic waste sa Quezon City Biyernes ng madaling araw. Nagpa-Patrol, Lyza Aquino. TV Patrol, Biyernes, 15 Marso 2024

headline: Chocolate Hills resort 'an eyesore', agencies pointing fingers: Binay | ABS-CBN News
text:
Who's to blame for the construction of a swimming pool and resort at the famous Chocolate Hills of Bohol? Government agencies involved are pointing fingers, according to Senate tourism chair Nancy Binay.
In a statement, Binay said there have been several reports about the closure of Captain’s Peak Resort after a video of its swimming pool at the foot of the Chocolate Hills in the town of Sagbayan, Bohol went viral. 
"Walang gustong umako ng kasalanan, at medyo nagtuturuan na at naghuhugas ng kamay ang mga ahensyang directly involved in allowing the construction of a resort at Chocolate Hills," she said.
She said that based on documents, the management of Captain's Peak had already started the paperwork for the eventual construction of the resort way back in 2018 even before the COVID-19 pandemic.
"Kaya nagtataka tayo kung bakit sa loob ng anim na taon ay nailusot ang mga permits at tinuloy ang construction, which should not have been allowed in the first place," she said. 
The senator said local authorities and environment officials should have started regular inspections of the resort the minute construction started. 
She said the local government of Sagbayan admitted the Department of Environment and Natural Resources only advised Captain's Peak Resort to undergo voluntary temporary closure. 
"So, makikita nating malamya ang enforcement at yung term na 'voluntary closure' means 'optional', ibig sabihin the resort has a choice not to stop its operations," she said. 
She added: "The temporary closure does not remedy anything or reverse damages done to the environment. It is very clear that there were infractions and willful violations of law in all levels. The extent of environmental degradation is simply unacceptable--hindi pwedeng walang accountable dito."
Binay said she has filed a resolution asking the Senate committee on the environment, natural resources and climate change chaired by Sen. Cynthia Villar to investigate the resort. 
One thing the committee could look into, she said, is if the environment department or the province has a land use plan of the Chocolate Hills, considering that UNESCO recently designated Bohol province as a global geopark. 
This, after the Captain's Peak Resort management presented a transfer certificate of title and other documents proving that the area where the resort is located is privately owned. The management, however, admitted it did not secure an environment compliance certificate. 
Manager of controversial Chocolate Hills resort admits not being able to acquire environment certificate
"Kahit may titulo sila, kung ‘yung intent ba nilang structure ay pasok dun sa environmental clearance na kailangang kunin sa DENR…Ang sa akin napaka-eyesore nang hitsura. Ang sakit sa bangs…Para sa akin, dapat siyang buwagin," Binay said in a TeleRadyo Serbisyo interview. 
"Is it high time na magkaroon ng pondo ‘yung pamahalaan para itong mga titled lots ay bibilhin ng government para hindi siya ma-develop for whatever? 
"Nakakaawa din yung may-ari kasi nag invest siya ng malaki para dun sa property. Malaki din naman ‘yung nagastos. It’s his property pero dapat ‘yung government agencies should have said right from the start, hindi pwede," she added. 
For his part, geologist Dr. Mahar Lagmay said he will not delve into the legal issues surrounding the ownership of Captain's Peak resort but noted the construction will have an impact on the environment. 
"Mas aesthetic ang pananaw ko dito. Meron tayong napakaganda na bagay na Chocolate Hills... Pag tinignan mo 'yan, makiklita mo puro burol na inukit ng panahon over a period of 150 million years tapos lalagyan mo ng resort," he said in a separate interview.  
"Para kang may waterfalls...Tapos lalagyan mo ng bahay na may trapal. Masisira 'yung aesthetic value niya."

headline: COVID survivors, ibinahagi ang epekto ng pandemya | ABS-CBN News
text: Apat na taon na mula nang unang ideklara ang community quarantine o lockdown dahil sa COVID-19 pandemic. Napakaraming buhay ang nawala pero mas marami ang nalampasan ang kakaibang pagsubok, hindi lamang sa kalusugan kundi pati sa kabuhayan at uri ng pamumuhay ng bawa’t tao sa buong mundo, kabilang tayong mga Pilipino. Ang tanong, handa ba tayo sakaling magkaroon ng panibagong pandemya? Nagpa-Patrol, Arra Perez. TV Patrol, Biyernes, 15 Marso 2024

headline: Umano’y lider ng carnapping group, arestado sa Cavite | ABS-CBN News
text: Arestado ang umano’y lider ng isang carnapping group at ilang kasabwat nito sa Cavite. Modus umano ng mga suspek na rentahan ang sasakyan pero hindi na isasaulo at papalitan pa ng mga pekeng dokumento para maibenta. Nagpa-Patrol, Jeff Caparas. TV Patrol, Biyernes, 15 Marso 2024

headline: ‘Payday weekend’ traffic, asahan sa Metro Manila | ABS-CBN News
text: Asahan na ang posibleng traffic sa ilang bahagi ng kalakhang Maynila ngayong weekend. Kasunod ito ng abiso ng DOTr sa gagawing paglilipat, simula ngayong gabi, ng mga makinarya para sa ginagawang Metro Manila Subway Project. Pero kanina pa lang, ramdam na ng maraming motorista ang pagsisikip ng daloy ng trapiko. Nagpa-Patrol, Johnson Manabat. TV Patrol, Biyernes, 15 Marso 2024

headline: Lider umano ng 'rentangay' gang, arestado sa Cavite | ABS-CBN News
text:
Sa bisa ng warrant of arrest pinasok ng mga operatiba ng Highway Patrol Group Calabarzon ang isang hotel sa Molino, Bacoor, Cavite tanghali nitong Huwebes.
Inaresto ang umano'y lider ng isang carnapping group.
Natunton ang suspek sa timbre ng asset ng mga pulis at nang mismong huling nabiktima nito.
Nakuha mula sa suspek ang isang 9mm na baril at mga bala.
Sa imbestigasyon ng HPG cavite, sangkot sa "rentangay" modus ang suspek.
Ayon kay Policed Major Gegeon Merza, hepe ng HPG Cavite, "Itong suspek, rent scam involving motor vehicle. Through online, mayroong siyang mga kasamahan through online nagkakaroon ng paghihikayat sa mga nagpaparenta... Pagtapos nun saka nila itatakbo, aalisin yung mga conduction sticker at ito nga under investigation namin na merong mga OR-CR na pinepeke.”
Ayon pa sa HPG, kapag nagawan na ng mga pekeng dokumento, dito na maibebenta ng suspek ang sasakyan sa iba pang posibleng mabiktima na hindi masusuring mabuti ang mga pinekeng papeles nito.
Matagal nang pinaghahanap ng otoridad ang suspek dahil sa warrant of arrest nito sa kasong robbery with homicide sa Laguna at isa pang kaso ng carnapping sa Taguig. Inilabas ang mga warrant laban sa kanya noon pang 2020.
Ayon sa biktima, buwanan umano ang usapan na renta sa kanyang hinuhulugan sasakyan, at ang pangako, gagamitin lang umano sa mga personal na lakad at meetings.
Ayon sa biktima, “Nirent po yun is P60,000 a month, ngayon nag-downpayment po sila sa akin ng P20,000 so may balance pa po sila na P38,000 hindi ko na po hinabol yun ang hinabol ko na lang po yung sasakyan ko...  Sobrang importante po sa akin ng sasakyan siyempre po yung binabayad ko po eh, hinuhulugan ko po kasi ito.”
Sa tulong ng tracker, natunton niya sa isang subdivision sa General Trias ang sasakyan.
“May GPS po kasi iyan ki-nut nila yung GPS, ngayon na-track ko po yung expander dahil may natira pa pong isang GPS na hindi nila na-cut... nadetect ko po sa GPS, ngayon nalaman ko po kung sino yung may hawak unit ko yung Santi at yung Miguel, nantunton po namin dun kasi sila po may hawak ng susi ko eh, ayaw po nilang ibigay,” sabi pa ng biktima.
Dahil ayaw nang isauli ng mga rumenta ang sasakyan, dito na sila nagpatulong sa HPG.  Naaresto ang mga suspek, hanggang sa natunton din ang pinaka lider nila.
Babala naman ang awtoridad sa mga nagpaparenta ng sasakyan, “Maging maingat po ano, lalo na kung ang sasakyan ay di pa yan bayad sa bangko, huwag po nating ipaparenta violation po yan ng mortgage law, kung yan ay di pa bayad sa financing sa bank, huwag na huwag nating ipaparenta,” ayon kay Merza.
Desidido ang biktima na sampahan ng reklamo ang suspek na tumangging magbigay ng pahayag.
Mahaharap sa kasong carnapping at illegal possession of firearms and ammunition ang suspek habang patuloy na tinutugis ang iba pa umanong kasabwat nito.

headline: 5 'budol-budol' suspek arestado sa QC | ABS-CBN News
text:
Arestado ng Station 4 ng Quezon City Police District (QCPD) ang limang suspek na nambudol umano sa isang senior citizen sa Novaliches, Quezon City.
Sa imbistigasyon ng QCPD, naglalakad ang biktima nang lapitan ng unang suspek sa Bgy. Nagkaisang Nayon, Novaliches.  
Sa imbestigasyon, sinabi ng isa sa suspek na kilala niya niya ang anak ng biktima, kaya't agad na nakuha umano ang loob ng senior citizen.
Inimbitahan pa niya ang biktima na sumakay sa sasakyan kung saan naroon naman ang ibang suspek.
Kinumbinsi nila ang matanda na iwan ang kanyang singsing at pumunta sa bangko para mag withdraw ng pera — may freebies daw na makukuha ang  biktima pag nag withdraw siya ng pera — tulad ng TV at Refrigerator.
Pero tila natauhan ang biktima nang mag wi-withdraw na siya ng pera sa bangko.
DIto, nagpatulong na sa mga pulis, kaya't nang bumalik na ang biktima sa mga suspek, kunwari dala ang P200,000 na pera.
Doon na sila inaresto ng mga pulis ang lima.
Tumanggi naman ang mga suspek sa krimen at 'di raw nila kilala ang biktima.
“Hindi po namin siya nakakaharap po, Tinuro po kami bigla," SabI ng isa sa mga nahuling suspek.
 Nagpayo ang mga pulis na huwag maniniwala sa mga taong basta-basta na lang nakikilala.

headline: Acorda: Hamon sa PNP mabilis na pag-usad ng teknolohiya | ABS-CBN News
text:
Philippine National Police Chief Director General Benjamin Acorda Jr. sa isang panayam. Raya Capulong, ABS-CBN News
Sa loob ng 11 buwan, bilang Philippine National Police Chief, aminado si General Benjamin Acorda Jr. na nagkulang siya sa pagtutok sa isyu ng information communication technology sa bansa.
Sa eksklusibong panayam ng ABS-CBN News kay Acorda, sinabi niyang sa kabila ng mga ginawa nilang hakbang, isang hamon sa kanila ang mabilis na pag-usad ng teknolohiya.
"Yung information communication technology talagang mabilis. How I wish we could really cope up with technology pero habang ginagawa nating yung hakbang towards a better organization that is at far with other countries, lumalakad din, nag-a-advance din ang technology and talagang it's hard to catch up with technology. So how I wish talagang we have more of it financially yung support, budget and of course more personnel na talagang tututok dyan," sabi ni Acorda.
Inamin ng heneral na kung natutukan niya ito ay maaring hindi masyadong tataas ang mga cybercrime cases.
"Yes because we all know during the last quarter nakita namin talagang tumaas yung statistics ng cybercrime but so far nag-a-adjust naman ang organization we conducted trainings and other capacity building, pati equipment down to the regional level," dagdag ni Acorda.
Nitong Pebrero nagpahayag ng pagkabahala ang PNP sa patuloy na pagtaas ng cybercrime cases sa bansa.
Sa datos ng PNP Anti-Cybercrime Group, sa mga naireport na cybercrime cases, pinaka maraming nabiktima ng online scamming na dumoble ang bilang mula sa naitalang 7,208 cases noong 2022, pumalo ito sa mahigit 14 thousand cases noong 2023.
Habang ngayong Enero may nareport na 1,044 cases na.
Sinundan ito ng identity theft at cyber libel.  
Kasunod nito ipinag-utos ni Pangulong Ferdinand Marcos Jr. sa PNP na palakasin ang kampanya laban sa cybercrime at paigtingin ang cybersecurity system sa bansa.
Inamin din ng opisyal na isa ang ilegal na droga sa mahirap na resolbahing problema sa bansa.
"Kasama na yung drugs... especially on the drugs medyo complicated. Ang daming temptation on the ground but through yung close monitoring and isa nga yung active coordination yung pagsusumbong ng mamamayan, yung mga anomalya biglang makita mo na parang nag-shy away yung ibang may tendency to do wrong. Nag-alangan na yung ibang kapulisan," ani ni Acorda.
Kasabay din ng kanyang pagkakatalaga noong Abril, sinalo ni Acorda ang mga problemang may kaugnayan sa ilegal na droga kung saaan nasangkot ang ilang matataas na opisyal ng PNP, ang internal cleansing at drug war.
Giit niya, nahirapan din siyang makuha ang tiwala ang publiko.
"It's how really to gain the trust and confidence of the Filipino people, yun ang parang nakita nating challenge. Although alam natin kasi galing ako sa Directorate for Intelligence so I know the figures kung gaano kakaunti lang naman yung gumagawa ng demeanors but still we have to relay it to the people and so yun ang parang number challenge specially when I assumed naging sensational yung 990 kilos, so that was a big challenge for me and for the organization," sabi ni Acorda.
Bukod dito, nasangkot din ang ilang mga pulis sa iba't-ibang insidente, tulad ng pagkakapatay sa binatilyong si Jemboy Baltazar noong August 2, ang pagkakapatay ng isang pulis sa isang binatilyo sa Rodriguez Rizal noong August 20, ang pagpasok at pagnanakaw umano ng mga pulis sa bahay ng retiradong guro sa Imus, Cavite noong August 2, at mga pulis na sangkot sa robbery extortion sa Maynila at Bacoor Cavite.
Hindi man mabagsik sa kanyang pananalita, sinabi ni Acorda na pinairal nya ang pagiging democratic leader.
Pero giit niya, naging transparent at umaaksyon aniya siya kapag napatutunayang may nagkamali o umabusong pulis.
"I treated the PNP organization as my family and pag may gumagawa ng kalokohan talagang nasasaktan ako because it tarnishes the image and the name of the organization. Iyung ang unang papasok sa akin bakit nanaman, pinipilit nga natin baguhin yung pangalan natin, yung image natin bakit may gumagawa nanaman ng ganon. Immediately may mga actions naman na gagawin, investigation kung kailangan ilipat nating yung mga pulis inililipat naman," sabi ni Acorda.
Ikinwento naman ni Acorda na sa mahigit 37 taon niya sa PNP, maraming beses na aniya nyang naisip umalis sa organisasyon.
Hindi man idinetalye, iginiit nyang ito'y dahil sa pagkadismaya niya sa sistema.
"There are instances I wanted to quit but then sige pagbigyan ko 'to (why Sir? as is aalis ka na sa PNP?) Yah (why?) frustrations, (frustrations?) the system and everything...But everytime I wanted to shed my uniform there's always a reason na parang hindi ko na itutuloy," paliwanag ni Acorda.
Si Acorda ay miyembro ng Philippine Military Academy "Sambisig" Class of 1991.
Naging pinuno siya ng PNP Directorate for Intelligence bago maitalaga bilang ika-29 na PNP Chief.
Nakatakda sana syang magretiro noong December 3 pero pinalawig ni Pangulong Marcos ang kanyang termino bilang PNP Chief hanggang Marso 31.
Bagamat lubos na nagpagpapasalamat aniya siya sa Pangulo, inamin ng opisyal na mas naging mahirap para sa kanya ang kanyang extension sa PNP.
"That is the hardest part na maextend kasi ibig sabihin nandoon yung tiwala well yung pagiging Chief PNP nandoon na yung tiwala but then there are other officers na pwede mai-appoint naman, bakit na-extend. So tagalang it will be an effort na ipakita mo na you really deserve that extension parang yung ang naging challenge parang everday you wanted to convince the other na hindi nagkamali ang Presidente," sabi ni Acorda.
Nang matanong kung bukas pa siya sa isa pang extension sa kanyang termino bilang PNP Chief, tumanggi itong magkumento.
Payo naman niya sa sususnod na maitatalangang PNP Chief na parating gawin kung ano ang tama.
"It's really a complicated organization but one thing for sure that what will make his job easy is to make sure kung ano yung nararapat, just do what is right," ani ni Acorda.
Nais ni Acorda na maglaaan ng mas maraming oras sa kanyang pamilya kapag nagretiro sa PNP. 
Wala siyang balak pumasok sa politika pero bukas naman aniya sya sa posibilidad na maglingkod sa mga departamento o ano mang posisyon na ialok sa kanya sa gobyerno.
Sa kanyang nalalapit na pagretiro sa Marso 31, nais ni Acorda na bigyaang diin ang kahalagan ng pagiging makabayan.
"In their small little way sa pagiging pulis kung may paraan silang alam nilang pwedeng gawin na maipakita sa taong bayan at pwedeng gawin nila, every opportunity that they have gawin nilang kagalanggalang ang mga pulis. Ganon ko gusto maalala ang ating kapulisan... one thing that I want to emphasize right now is patriotism, yung pagmamahal sa ating bansa because of all the things that I'm seeing nakikita ko talaga kailangan the only thing that can save this country talaga from further deterioration is we will come up with one definition and one way of expressing na mahalin yung bansa natin," sabi ni Acorda.
Nagpasalamat si Acorda sa suportang ibinigay sa kanya ng mamamayan at tiniyak na tapat na naglilingkod at laging handang magbigay serbisyo publiko ang mga pulis.

headline: CHED looking into transgender student being forced to get haircut | ABS-CBN News
text:
Members of the LGBTQI community and supporters open a rainbow crosswalk along the Academic Oval in UP Diliman. The crosswalk aims to serve as a symbol of inclusivity and diversity within and outside the university. Mark Demayo, ABS-CBN News
MANILA (UPDATED) - The Commission on Higher Education is looking into a controversial viral video that showed a transgender student of the Eulogio “Amang” Rodriguez Institute of Science and Technology (EARIST) in Manila being forced to cut her hair just so she can enroll at the school.
CHED Chairman Prospero De Vera said that while such policies regarding students are individually determined by the universities, these institutions should adjust rules to comply with existing laws, such as laws prohibiting discrimination, and the safe spaces act.
“Iniimbestigahan na natin iyan ‘no. Inutusan ko ang ating Executive Director, si Atty. Cindy Jaro na tingnan itong isyung ito. Ang patakaran kasi tungkol sa students ‘no, iyong kanilang haircut, uniform, et cetera – iyan ay ginagawa ng indibidwal na mga pamantasan, walang CHEd rules kasi bahagi iyan ng kanilang exercise of their administration of higher education,” De Vera said in a televised briefing.
(We are already investigating that. I have instructed our Executive Director, Atty. Cindy Jaro to look into this issue. The policy about students, their haircut, uniform, et cetera - that is done by individual universities. There are no CHED rules because that is part of their exercise of their administration of higher education.)
“Ang pakiusap natin sa mga pamantasan ay maging sensitive at pag-aralan ang compliance nila sa mga batas na naipasa tulad ng mga batas na pinagbabawal ang diskriminasyon, ang batas sa safe spaces kailangan ang kanilang mga patakaran sa pamantasan ay angkop o consistent doon sa mga batas na kailangang i-implement ng lahat – iyon ang kailangang pag-aralan nila,” he added.
(Our plea to the universities is to be sensitive and study their compliance with the laws that have been passed such as the laws that prohibit discrimination, the law on safe spaces, it is necessary that their university policies are appropriate or consistent with those laws that everyone needs to implement – that's what they need to study.)
"So, I urge our schools to have very open dialogue with our students to see what they need, what they are saying and adjust the policies of the school consistent with laws that we must observe – iyon ang kailangang gawin ng ating mga pamantasan dito," De Vera added.
(So, I urge our schools to have very open dialogue with our students to see what they need, what they are saying, and adjust the policies of the school consistent with laws that we must observe – that's what our universities have to do here.) 
De Vera also called on affected students to first seek administrative remedies within the school, and then later lodge a formal complaint within the commission if the school fails to address the issue. 
He assured them that he will personally look into their complaint.
“Ngayon kung mayroong mga estudyanteng naagrabyado – first, they must exhaust the administrative remedies available in the school at kapag sa kanilang palagay iyong kanilang eskuwelahan ay hindi pa rin sila tinutulungan, kayo po ay magreklamo sa Komisyon at ako po mismo ang titingin sa inyong reklamo,” the official said.
(Now if there are students who are aggrieved - first, they must exhaust the administrative remedies available in the school and if they think that their school is still not helping them, you can complain to the Commission and I myself will look into your complaint.)
In a separate statement, De Vera said CHED has facilitated a dialogue between EARIST and the affected students.
After the discussion between both parties, it was decided that all students of EARIST will be allowed to enroll regardless of the length of their hair.
EARIST also agreed to suspend the questionable provisions of the student handbook, and consult with all students in drafting “new and more inclusive guidelines” for the students.
CHED, for its part, will also organize workshops among higher education institutions to share “best practices in inclusivity and gender sensitivity”.
“I urge our higher education institutions to ensure that their student policies are consistent with requirements of law and open lines of constant communication with students. Their voices must be heard and considered in the crafting of policies,” De Vera added.

headline: DOH says 'ready' if another pandemic breaks out | ABS-CBN News
text:
Members of the hospital housekeeping staff at the Amang Rodriguez Hospital in Marikina City dispose healthcare waste into a containment facility on September 30, 2020. Jonathan Cellona, ABS-CBN News/file 
Healthcare worker Salome Ejes still sheds tears every time she recounts her experience of being infected with COVID-19 thrice at the height of the pandemic. 
"Alam mo iyong feeling na, 'Makakauwi ba ako nang buhay?'" she told ABS-CBN News. "Iyong second COVID ko, ang sakit huminga. Nangangatog ka; wala kang panlasa, wala kang pang-amoy."
"Noong time na naka-survive, nagpasalamat talaga ako sa Diyos. We see to it po na iyong family laging may contact kahit sa kaunting panahon," she added. 
Ejes still works as a ward clerk at the Philippine Heart Center.
WHO urges pandemic accord in 2024
How the COVID pandemic changed the travel industry
But it's a different story for Larry Muñoz, whose livelihood had to adjust because of the pandemic. 
From being a construction worker, he learned how to cook and is now a street food vendor.
"Kapag malakasan, hindi naman bababa sa P8,000 ang kita sa araw-araw. Tsaka hindi rin bababa sa P5,000 [araw-araw]," he said. 
His biggest takeaway from the pandemic? 
"Mag-negosyo na lang talaga para makakain... Para matawid gutom sa pamilya," Muñoz said. 
It was on March 15, 2020 when a lockdown was first implemented in NCR due to the COVID-19 pandemic, which was then followed suit by the rest of the country. 
Four years since that day, the Department of Health (DOH) said it is ready should there be another pandemic. 
"The DOH is categorically ready, that is correct. As to how ready, no one in this world is 100 percent ready. Even the WHO itself was not prepared when COVID-19 came out... We cannot be caught, kumbaga, flatfooted, na parang nakatunganga tayo tapos nagre-relax. Hindi p’wede, dapat ready tayo. Insofar as the Philippines and the DOH is concerned, we are ready," said DOH Asec. Albert Domingo. 
PRIMARY HEALTH CARE 
Aside from hospitals, the DOH is also preparing primary health care facilities so that patients have somewhere to go to in case of another health emergency, such as a pandemic. 
"Moving forward, we have learned our lessons and we now know better. The UHC Act is still our reference, and it is the order of President Marcos to strengthen primary health care to prepare us for the next pandemic," DOH Sec. Ted Herbosa said in a statement. 
Domingo explained this also encompasses the whole health system. 
"‘Pag sinabi nating health system, iyong bilang ng kama, bilang ng ospital, bilang ng doktor. Hindi tayo dapat palaging umasa lang sa ospital," he said.
"So that was a very strong lesson, moving forward from the pandemic to prepare for the next one. Kasi it's not a matter of 'if', but a matter of 'when'. Iyong sinasabi na 'disease X' - ibig sabihin hindi pa natin alam. Pero mangyayari iyan. We have to be ready with facilities, not the hospital level but at the level of primary care," he added.
Based on data compiled by the ABS-CBN Investigative and Research Group, the Philippines recorded its highest daily COVID cases of 38,775 on January 15, 2022, which has dipped to an average of 36 cases daily during the week of February 27 to March 4, 2024.
A total of 179,046,746 doses of vaccines have been administered, as of March 19, 2023 based on the latest update of the National COVID-19 Vaccination dashboard
According to the DOH, only 10 percent of ICU beds for COVID are occupied, and 11 percent for non-ICU beds. 
"Out of the 1,185 dedicated COVID-19 ICU beds available today, only 115 (10%) were occupied, while only 1,119 (11%) of the 10,097 dedicated COVID-19 non-ICU beds were in use. There were 156 severe and critical cases admitted in various hospitals due to COVID-19," the agency said. 
All of these have contributed to why the World Health Organization (WHO) is no longer treating COVID as a global health emergency since May 5, 2023 - while the Philippines lifted the public health emergency on July 21, 2023. 
But for strategies like wearing of face mask, physical distancing, isolation, lockdown, and school closure – which is the most effective?
"What is always effective is the face mask. The psychological cause and the extra cause is minimal and smaller than other things, and the impact is large… What is always not effective is school closure... The students who do not go to school after closure, they actually indeed infect somewhere else: at home, with their friends... The disease burden for the young generation is low, and the learning loss for after school closure is huge," said Hyuncheol Bryant Kim, Associate Professor of Hong Kong University of Science and Technology's Department of Economics during an Asian Development Bank (ADB) forum Thursday. 
HEALTH CARE WORKERS
Can health care workers still take on another pandemic? 
"Hindi po tayo ready, lalong-lalo ngayon na may exodus ang ating health care workers, especially nurses, midwives, nursing attendants at iba pang health care workers. Hindi po tayo ready sa usapin ng human resources," said Robert Mendoza, national president of the Alliance of Health Workers. 
"Prior to the pandemic, meron na talaga tayong chronic understaffing... Kung wala na talagang matitirang health workers sa ating bansa dahil sa kababaan ng sahod, possible babagsak ang ating health care system," he added.
Mendoza is dismayed that nothing has changed on issues hounding the health care delivery system.
"Kasi alam natin nandyan pa rin ang chronic understaffing, mababa ang sahod, demoralized pa rin ang health workers, delayed ang benepisyo... Talagang hindi siya sapat sa pagbibigay ng libreng serbisyong pangkalusugan," he said.
Meantime, Domingo guaranteed help for their ranks.
"We are looking at better working conditions... DOH is working with the DBM [Department of Budget and Management], and lately the Commission on Audit. Ano bang mga balakid bakit hindi pa mabigay iyong health emergency allowance? Kasi sabi nga namin sa ospital, kailangang gawan ng paraan... ‘Pag buhay ng tao ang pinag-uusapan, sino bang sumasaklolo? Ang health care workers. So tulungan natin sila," he said.
RELATED VIDEO
